current_focus: Track a Pareto frontier across reconstruction quality and sparsity/cost. Treat K=128 as one anchor point, not the only success criterion. Prefer experiments that add non-dominated tradeoff points, including smaller-K runs that accept some FVU increase when they improve the overall frontier.

failure_patterns.pattern: A low-rank trunk plus two residual sparse refinement passes (`lowrank_two_stage_residual`) should improve the low-K Pareto lane versus plain `lowrank_residual` by spending the same total K on sequential residual cleanup, especially around the EF=12 regime.

lowrank_two_stage_residual.design_hypothesis: Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 2 to 1 will test the endpoint of this family's ultra-low-K tail and determine whether it can still produce a non-dominated frontier point at the minimum support budget.
next_steps: If `K=1` stays competitive, switch focus to a routed variant around K=16-24 to see whether support allocation can bend the mid-K frontier further.
If `K=1` degrades sharply, shift back to the 16-24 regime and test a routed variant for better support allocation.
If `K=1` is borderline but interesting, keep the family fixed and tune stage allocation rather than changing the broader recipe.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: A low-rank trunk plus two residual sparse refinement passes (`lowrank_two_stage_residual`) should improve the low-K Pareto lane versus plain `lowrank_residual` by spending the same total K on sequential residual cleanup, especially around the EF=12 regime.
Probe whether `lowrank_two_stage_residual` can add a new smaller-K Pareto point by reducing K from 32 to 24 at the same EF=12 recipe, accepting some FVU increase if the K reduction is non-dominated.

`lowrank_two_stage_residual` may remain on the Pareto frontier at K=16, even with higher FVU than K=24, if the low-rank trunk plus sequential residual cleanup preserves enough quality to create a new non-dominated smaller-K point.
recent_rounds.termination_reason: Variable isolation violated: Multiple primary variables: architecture, k, ef, lr

recent_rounds.hypothesis: Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 24 to 16 will test whether this family can extend the Pareto frontier to a smaller-support point without unacceptable FVU degradation.

Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 16 to 8 will test whether this family can add a further non-dominated low-support point on the Pareto frontier, accepting some FVU increase if the tradeoff remains competitive.

Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 8 to 4 will test whether this family can still add a non-dominated ultra-low-K point, completing the current low-K sweep before changing architecture.

Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 4 to 2 will test whether this family remains Pareto-relevant in the extreme low-K regime, identifying where its low-support frontier breaks down.

Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 2 to 1 will test the endpoint of this family's ultra-low-K tail and determine whether it can still produce a non-dominated frontier point at the minimum support budget.

recent_insights: NOTE: Integrated new architecture family `lowrank_two_stage_residual` into config validation and `_get_sae_class` dispatch.
NOTE: Implemented `LowRankTwoStageResidualSparseCoder` as low-rank trunk + two sequential residual sparse passes with shared decoder.
NOTE: Exact smoke test passed for `lowrank_two_stage_residual` at K=32, EF=12 with forward/backward.
r1 lowrank_two_stage_residual edit_sae_code k32 -> crash [crash]
NOTE: Round 2 shifts to a parameter-only probe on `lowrank_two_stage_residual` to test the K dimension directly.
NOTE: Primary comparison target is whether K=24 yields a non-dominated FVU/K tradeoff relative to the K=32 anchor for the same family and EF.
r2 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k24 -> keep fvu=0.09643366932868958
NOTE: K=24 EF=12 `lowrank_two_stage_residual` produced a keep-worthy point at FVU 0.09643, so the next informative move is to map the same family lower on the K axis.
NOTE: Round 3 tests whether `lowrank_two_stage_residual` can extend the frontier to K=16 without recipe drift.
r3 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k? -> policy_reject (Variable isolation violated: Multiple primary variables: architecture, k, ef, lr)
NOTE: Round 4 should be a pure K-isolation run on `lowrank_two_stage_residual`, fixing the prior variable-isolation failure by explicitly pinning architecture, EF, optimizer, LR, and AUXK_ALPHA.
NOTE: For low-K frontier mapping, treat `K=16` as the next decision point before considering more structural changes.
r3 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k16 -> keep fvu=0.10792168974876404
NOTE: Round 4 extends the controlled K sweep for `lowrank_two_stage_residual` from 16 to 8 with all other recipe fields pinned.

NOTE: The current low-K lane is yielding monotonic frontier points at K=32, 24, and 16, so the most informative next step is to test whether the same family remains Pareto-relevant at K=8 before trying a new architecture.
r4 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k8 -> keep fvu=0.12856262922286987
NOTE: Round 5 extends the controlled `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K sweep from 8 to 4 with all other recipe fields pinned.
NOTE: The family has produced keep-worthy points at K=32, 24, 16, and 8, so finishing the ultra-low-K tail is more informative than switching architectures immediately.
r5 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k4 -> keep fvu=0.15092788636684418
NOTE: Round 6 extends the controlled `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K sweep from 4 to 2 with all other recipe fields pinned.
NOTE: The family has remained keep-worthy at every lower-K step so far, so testing `K=2` is the highest-information continuation before branching to routed variants or new families.
r6 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k2 -> keep fvu=0.17282412946224213
NOTE: Round 7 extends the controlled `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K sweep from 2 to 1 with all other recipe fields pinned.

NOTE: The family has remained keep-worthy from K=32 down through K=2, so testing `K=1` is the cleanest way to locate the endpoint of its Pareto-relevant low-K tail before introducing architecture changes.
r7 lowrank_two_stage_residual param_only k1 -> keep fvu=0.17282412946224213
